pi_items.source: FITS header (TELESCOP='UIT', OBJECT='NGC4151') and NASA GSFC archive URL
setup_task.sh copies /opt/fits_samples/uit_galaxy_sample.fits to the expected path
Matches export_result.sh python verification script target path
Matches export_result.sh python verification script text file target

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real astronomical FITS data from the NASA Ultraviolet Imaging Telescope (UIT) representing galaxy NGC 4151. The target file is downloaded from NASA GSFC (UITfuv2582gc.fits) and renamed to uit_galaxy_sample.fits. Ground truth analysis using Python (matching the task's evaluation script logic) confirms the exact difference residual between the image and its 180-degree rotated counterpart should have a Mean of ~1.92e-15, a Standard Deviation of ~1.16e-14, a Minimum of 0.0, and a Maximum of ~4.22e-13. The verifier inside export_result.sh empirically evaluates the output against these exact constraints.
